Article Title: Amazon Web Services CEO Matt Garman ‘admits’ Iran war poses ongoing challenges; says: You know, there is not ...
Article Text:
AWS CEO sees 'promise' in Middle East

Amazon internal memo confirms some AWS zones unavailable

Poll Which region do you think will be most affected by the Iran war among the following? Middle East Europe North America Asia





Amazon Web Services CEO Matt Garman has reportedly accepted that the ongoing Iran war poses challenges for the world's biggest cloud service provider. Speaking to CNBC at the HumanX conference in San Francisco, AWS CEO Garman termed it a difficult situation and said that Amazon employees are working round the clock to prevent service disruption for its customers in the Middle East region. “It’s a really difficult situation, and we’re working incredibly hard,” Garman said. He added, “In fact, we have teams, 24/7, working to make sure that we can keep our infrastructure up for our customers in that region.”Amazon's cloud services have reported service disruption related to the Iran conflict in Bahrain and the UAE since early March. According to the AWS status page, dozens of AWS services in Bahrain and the United Arab Emirates continue to remain unavailable as the Iran war has entered sixth week. Last week, Iran’s Revolutionary Guard navy announced that it had targeted Amazon data center infrastructure in Bahrain.Garman maintained that the war is disruptive not just for the Middle East, but the entire global economy. “It’s obviously hugely disruptive for the global economy, as we’re all very dependent on energy, and also just distracting for industry, for us,” Garman said. “You know, there’s not short-term, immediate things, but it really is just the drag on the global economy that we have to think about.”Garman further added that technology is not the only industry seeing implications. “You just have to go further down the supply chain to find something, and so we’re not different than that,” he said.Garman, however, expressed optimism about the Middle East. “There’s a fantastic entrepreneurial spirit,” he said. “There’s a willingness to invest. And so our and my excitement about investing long term in that region is just as strong as it’s ever been.”A recent report in Big Technology, quoting an internal Amazon memo, claimed that Iranian strikes have rendered two Amazon Web Services availability zones “hard down” in Dubai and Bahrain and the company expects them to be “unavailable for an extended period”.“These two regions continue to be impaired, and services should not expect to be operating with normal levels of redundancy and resiliency,” an internal memo read. “We are actively working to free and reserve as much capacity as possible in the region for customers, and services should be scaled to the minimal footprint required to support customer migration.”AWS has also reportedly advised customers to migrate their applications to alternate AWS Regions, and said that it had already helped a large number of users to do so.
Article Summary: Amazon's cloud services have reported service disruption related to the Iran conflict in Bahrain and the UAE since early March. Iran’s Revolutionary Guard navy announced that it had targeted Amazon data center infrastructure in Bahrain. Amazon employees are working round the clock to prevent service disruption for its customers in the Middle East region.

Article Title: NASA administrator Jared Isaacman thanks Elon Musk and Jeff Bezos, says those who criticise billionaire space travel are ‘outright wrong’ because…
Article Text:
‘Progress cannot be paused’







NASA administrator has defended and thanked the billionaires who have poured their personal fortunes into the space race. Jared Isaacman, who was confirmed as NASA administrator late last year, slammed those who argue that wealthy individuals like Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos and Sir Richard Branson should be directing their resources toward problems on Earth rather than rockets pointed at the sky.“I'm grateful for folks like Elon Musk, and Jeff Bezos, and Sir Richard Branson that have put their resources on the line for a capability for the good of all humankind right now,” Isaacman told Politico even as four astronauts made history aboard NASA’s Artemis II mission, travelling farther from Earth than any humans before them.When the interviewer raised the views of UN Secretary General António Guterres who in 2021 criticised billionaires for “joyriding to space while millions go hungry on Earth”, Isaacman said, “I think they're just outright wrong, and ill-informed and going for headlines. It's such a bad take.”.Isaacman’s argues that waiting to solve every problem on Earth before pushing forward with space exploration is a false choice. He also drew a parallel with the rollout of mobile phone networks in the 1980s, a technology that also faced sceptics at the time. Space exploration, he argued, carries a long-term transformational potential that is impossible to fully appreciate from the present moment.“If we concentrate all of our resources on the problems and hardships of the day, there is no progress. You don't hit pause on progress,” Isaacman said, adding, “We should be grateful for their [billionaires’] contributions, and do the other things to make life better here on Earth,” he said.Isaacman is a member of the billionaire space club he is defending. In 2021, he led Inspiration4, the first all-civilian orbital mission, aboard a SpaceX Dragon capsule, flying higher than the suborbital flights that Bezos and Branson completed that same year.In 2024, he went further still, becoming the first civilian ever to conduct a spacewalk during SpaceX's five-day Polaris mission, venturing outside the spacecraft approximately 400 miles above Earth for around ten minutes to test SpaceX’s EVA spacesuit.Alongside his space ventures, Isaacman runs Draken International, which supplies tactical fighter aircraft to the US military and its allies — making him one of the more unconventional figures ever to lead NASA.
Article Summary: Jared Isaacman, who was confirmed as NASA administrator late last year, slammed those who argue that wealthy individuals like Elon Musk, Jeff Bezos and Sir Richard Branson should be directing their resources toward problems on Earth rather than rockets pointed at the sky. ‘We should be grateful for their [billionaires’] contributions, and do the other things to make life better here on Earth,’ he said.

Article Title: Infosys’ early bet on Whoop nears $100 million as wearable firm eyes IPO
Article Text:
Infosys has hired executive search firm Egon Zehnder to identify and shortlist possible external candidates.

Poll What do you think is the biggest challenge for Infosys in scaling startup investments? Market competition Integration with existing services Financial risk Talent acquisition





Bengaluru: One of Infosys’ earliest startup bets, Whoop, is now estimated to be worth nearly $100 million, underscoring a standout investment from its $500 million innovation fund. Infosys is learnt to hold about a 1% stake in the wearables firm.Whoop was valued at $10.1 billion in April following a $575 million funding round and is targeting an IPO within the next 18–24 months. The company has over 2.5 million members globally. In 2025, bookings more than doubled (up 103% year-on-year) to a $1.1 billion run rate, while turning cash flow positive. It is also hiring over 600 employees worldwide to support R&D and international expansion.Infosys first invested $3 million in Whoop in 2015 under former CEO Vishal Sikka. Since then, its exposure has increased, with the investment valued at nearly Rs 130 crore as of FY25. Emails sent to Infosys and Whoop did not elicit a response till press time.HfS Research CEO Phil Fersht said the investment reflects a broader push during the Sikka era to make strategic bets on digital platforms and emerging ecosystems, moving beyond traditional services.“What’s notable is not just the financial upside, but the strategic intent. A $3 million investment translating into exposure to a multibillion-dollar company shows services firms can create real enterprise value by embedding themselves in platform growth stories,” he said.However, he added that the key challenge is scaling such bets. “One success does not make a venture strategy. The opportunity lies in turning these into a repeatable growth engine aligned with AI-led services transformation.”Peter Bendor-Samuel, founder and executive chairman of Everest Group, said such windfalls are unlikely to materially move Infosys’ stock. “Strategically, it makes more sense to invest in startups that could disrupt tech services. The real test is whether these bets shape Infosys’ long-term growth trajectory,” he said.
Article Summary: Infosys has hired executive search firm Egon Zehnder to identify and shortlist possible external candidates. Infosys is learnt to hold about a 1% stake in the wearables firm. Whoop was valued at $10.1 billion in April following a $575 million funding round.

Article Title: Quote of the day by Mark Zuckerberg: “Treat your employees right, so they won't use your Internet to search for a new job.”
Article Text:
Quote of the day by Mark Zuckerberg: “Treat your employees right, so they won't use your Internet to search for a new job.”

“Treat your employees right, so they won't use your Internet to search for a new job.”

Quote of the day by Mark Zuckerberg



“Treat your employees right, so they won't use your Internet to search for a new job".”



What the quote by Mark Zuckerberg really means in simple terms



Why this quote matters more in today’s world



The workplace is no longer just about work



A closer look at Meta Platforms and its work culture



Respect and recognition: the foundation of employee satisfaction



The silent shift: When employees start looking elsewhere



Technology has changed everything



Mark Zuckerberg's other famous quotes



“The biggest risk is not taking any risk.”

“Move fast and break things. Unless you are breaking stuff, you are not moving fast enough.”

“People don’t care about what you say, they care about what you build.”

“Ideas don’t come out fully formed. They only become clearer as you work on them.”

A reminder for organisations of all sizes



Why this quote by Mark Zuckerberg continues to resonate







In today's fast-paced work world, where job openings are only a click away, keeping employees happy is more important than ever. People don't just look at a company's products or profits anymore; they also look at how they treat their employees. Mark Zuckerberg 's short and sharp quote sums up this truth in a way that is both simple and useful:The line may sound funny at first, but it is a serious truth about today's workplaces. Job portals, professional networks, and company reviews are all easy for employees to find these days. They don't have to wait long to look for other options if they feel unhappy, unappreciated, or undervalued. This quote makes a simple but important point: people are more likely to stay with a company and grow with it if they feel respected and supported at work.This quote is very clear. It shows how treating employees well can help keep them. People are less likely to think about leaving their jobs when they feel safe, respected, and valued there.The phrase "using your Internet" shows how simple it is to look for a new job. In the past, finding a job took time, work, and planning.It can happen quietly during a lunch break or even over the phone in just a few minutes. This change means that companies have to make sure their employees are happy every day, not just once in a while.The quote isn't just about keeping workers from quitting. It's about making it so they don't want to leave in the first place.Over the years, workplaces have changed a lot. People used to stay in the same job for a long time, sometimes for their whole career. Stability was important, and it wasn't common to change jobs.Things are very different now. Workers know more about their choices. They don't just care about salary; they also care about things like the work culture, respect, flexibility, and chances for growth. More and more people are working from home and using digital platforms, which has made it easier for them to look for jobs in other cities and even other countries.Companies can't just rely on pay packages to keep their workers in this situation. The overall experience of working for a company is very important. This is exactly what Zuckerberg's quote means in a way that everyone can understand.One big change in the last few years is how people feel about their jobs. It's not just a place to get things done anymore. People spend a lot of time there, talk to each other, and work on their careers.Because of this, workers want more than just orders and deadlines. They want to feel like they belong. They want to know that their work is important and that people notice what they do.Dissatisfaction starts to grow when these expectations aren't met. It might not be clear right away, but over time, it can cause people to lose interest. This is when workers often start looking for other jobs, sometimes without saying anything about their worries.Mark Zuckerberg is the co-founder and CEO of Meta Platforms, which is one of the most important tech companies in the world. The company has always put a lot of emphasis on new ideas, speed, and working together.Meta is known for promoting open communication, which means that employees are often given the chance to share their thoughts and ideas. The business has also put money into making workplaces that encourage creativity and productivity.Every business has its own problems, but the main point is still clear: businesses that care about their employees are more likely to keep them. This is very similar to what Zuckerberg said.The quote's main idea is simple: people want to be treated well. This doesn't always mean big rewards or benefits. A lot of the time, it's about how you treat people every day and basic respect.Employees are more likely to stay interested in their work when they feel like their opinions are valued. Even small acts of recognition can make a big difference. A positive environment is created by recognising hard work, giving helpful feedback, and making sure that decisions are fair.On the other hand, not getting recognition can make you angry. Employees may start to wonder where they fit in the company if they feel like their work isn't being noticed. This is where most people start looking for jobs.One of the most important things about this quote is that it shows how job searching often happens without anyone knowing. Employees don't always say they're unhappy. In a lot of cases, they keep doing their jobs while slowly looking into other options.It can be hard for businesses to notice this quiet time. There may be small signs, like less participation or less excitement, but people often miss them.Most of the time, an employee has been thinking about quitting for a while before they actually do it. This is why it's important to stay involved and talk to each other. Taking care of problems early can stop things like this from happening.The quote's mention of the internet is especially relevant right now. Finding a job is now faster, easier, and more accessible than ever, thanks to technology.Employees can now look at job listings, apply for jobs, and talk to recruiters in just a few minutes. They can look into new job opportunities without leaving their current jobs, thanks to professional networking sites.Because it's so easy to get to, dissatisfaction can quickly turn into action. So, businesses need to make sure that their employees feel valued every day, not just during performance reviews.The point of this quote isn't just for big companies or the tech industry. It works for businesses of all sizes and in all fields.The principle stays the same, no matter how big or small the business is. People are more likely to stay committed when they feel respected and supported. People who feel like they aren't being heard or valued are more likely to leave when they have the chance.Making the workplace better doesn't always mean making big changes. A lot of the time, it's about being consistent in how you act, making fair decisions, and being clear when you talk.This quote is easy to remember, which is why it stands out. It doesn't use complicated words or ideas that aren't clear. Instead, it shows a real situation in a way that is easy to understand.Most people can relate to it because they have been through it or seen it happen to other people. It shows that people are more likely to stay where they feel appreciated.This message is even more important now that people can easily move jobs and find new ones.At first, Mark Zuckerberg's quote about treating employees well may seem light, but it has a serious message about today's workplaces. It shows how the way employees are treated affects their decision to stay or leave.In today's digital world, where people can look for jobs right away, companies can't afford to ignore how happy their employees are. It's no longer optional to have respect, recognition, and a good place to work. They are very important.Organisations can build stronger teams and keep employees from looking for work elsewhere by focusing on people and creating a culture of trust and support. The idea is easy to understand, but it can have a big effect.
Article Summary: Quote of the day by Mark Zuckerberg: “Treat your employees right, so they won't use your Internet to search for a new job” In today's fast-paced work world, keeping employees happy is more important than ever. Mark Zuckerberg's short and sharp quote sums up this truth in a way that is both simple and useful.

Article Title: Foldable iPhone 'fears' wipe millions from Apple market value, stock regains after ‘on track’ report
Article Text:
Reputed Apple analyst Ming-Chi Kuo has come up with his prediction of Apples product lineup for the year 2021. Along with changes to the Mac and iPad lineup Kuo predicts that Apple will launch its first ever augmented reality device this year.

Reinventing iPhone with foldable model





iPhone fans, your wait for Apple's first-ever foldable iPhone is reportedly not getting longer. A recent report in Nikkei Asia claimed that Apple is facing manufacturing issues with its foldable iPhone and Apple may not launch it with iPhone 18 series in September 2026. According to a report in Bloomberg, Apple's first foldable phone is on track to arrive during the company’s normal iPhone launch period later this year, rebutting concerns about major manufacturing snags. The report quoting people with knowledge of the matter said that Apple is scheduled to introduce the foldable model in September alongside the iPhone 18 Pro and Pro Max. Apple’s phones typically hit store shelves the week after they’re unveiled, which is traditionally the third week of September.On Tuesday (April 7), after the Nikkei Asia report fueled concerns about the delay in the foldable iPhone launch, Apple shares fell as much as 5.1%, wiping millions from Apple market value. A Nikkei Asia report said that Apple was facing challenges in the engineering test phase of the phone that threatened to push back the production and shipment schedule.However, post Bloomberg report Apple shares pared their losses.The stock was down about 2.7% to $251.88 as of 1:04 p.m. in New York on Tuesday.The iPhone road map has been a priority for Apple management, including hardware chief John Ternus, who is widely speculated to be a top contender to eventually replace CEO Tim Cook. Foldable iPhone is one of the biggest launches from the company in years as well as most-awaited by Apple fans. With the foldable model, Apple is seeking to expand the iPhone line with new designs, pricier models and enhanced features. The company plans to take on Samsung and China-based smartphone makers, which have offered foldable options for years.Analysts see the foldable iPhone as step two of Apple's three-year plan to reinvent the look and feel of its flagship device. In 2025, Apple introduced overhauled iPhone Pro and Pro Max models and a thinner version called the iPhone Air. The company is also working on a separate iPhone revamp in 2027 to mark the product’s 20th anniversary.Design of Apple iPhone Fold or iPhone Ultra or ...The latest chatter around Apple’s first foldable, or rather more specifically on what it might be called suggests iPhone Ultra naming. According to a Weibo tipster @DigitalChatStation, Apple’s upcoming wide-format foldable may actually launch as the iPhone Ultra. Some earlier reports, from different sources, have claimed that the foldable iPhone may be called or iPhone Fold. Apple engineers reportedly believe they’ve solved problems with screen quality and overall durability, two long-running flaws with phones in this category. That includes making the display’s crease less visible when it’s unfolded.The so-called iPhone Ultra or iPhone Fold is speculated to sport a 5.5-inch display when closed, and a 7.8-inch display when open, plus a super thin design with minimal crease and a durable hinge. The goal reportedly is to be better usability both when folded and unfolded.Apple is said to be planning to update its iOS operating system to make iPhone apps look more like iPad software on the new device. The product’s price, meanwhile, is expected to cross the $2,000 threshold.
Article Summary: Apple's first foldable phone is on track to arrive during the company’s normal iPhone launch period later this year, rebutting concerns about major manufacturing snags. A recent report in Nikkei Asia claimed that Apple is facing manufacturing issues with its foldable iPhone and Apple may not launch it with iPhone 18 series in September 2026.

Article Title: As Intel joins SpaceX Terafab project, US chipmaker’s CEO Lip Bu-Tan praises Elon Musk: ‘This is exactly…’
Article Text:
Intel’s role in Elon Musk’s mega-factory



What is Elon Musk’s Terafab Project







Intel has officially joined forces with Elon Musk’s ‘mega company’: SpaceX, Tesla and xAI, to help build “Terafab,” a massive new semiconductor manufacturing project. As a part of the tie-up, the US chipmaker will bring its expertise in designing, fabricating and packaging high-performance chips to the table. Following the announcement, Intel CEO Lip Bu-Tan publicly praised Musk’s ambitious vision for the future of chipmaking.Intel said that it hosted Musk at its facilities over the weekend to solidify the partnership. According to the US chipmaker, the collaboration will focus on “refactoring” how silicon fabrication works. The ultimate goal is to help Terafab achieve a production rate of 1 Terawatt (TW) of computing power per year, which will be used to fuel the next generation of artificial intelligence (AI) and robotics.“Intel is proud to join the Terafab project with SpaceX, xAI, and Tesla to help refactor silicon fab technology. Our ability to design, fabricate, and package ultra-high-performance chips at scale will help accelerate Terafab’s aim to produce 1 TW/year of compute to power future advances in AI and robotics. It was fun hosting Elon Musk at Intel this past weekend!” Intel said in a post on X.Bu-Tan shared Intel’s announcement, and praises Musk for his “proven track record of reimagining entire industries,”“This is exactly what is needed in semiconductor manufacturing today.Terafab represents a step change in how silicon logic, memory, and packaging will get built in the future. Intel is proud to be a partner and work closely with Elon on this highly strategic project,” Lip Bu-Tan said in a post on X.Unveiled by Musk at a recent event in downtown Austin, Texas, Terafab is a joint venture operated by Tesla and SpaceX, with heavy support from Musk's artificial intelligence startup, xAI. Musk has previously described the vision as a “gigafactory, but way bigger.” According to the company, the facility will begin as an advanced technology fabrication plant capable of producing and testing various types of chips.“Even when we extrapolate the best-case scenario for chip production from our suppliers, it's still not enough,” Musk noted during Tesla's Annual General Meeting last year, adding, “We either build the Terafab or we don’t have the chips, and we need the chips, so we build the Terafab.”When operational, the Terafab will be churn out massive quantities of Tesla’s highly anticipated fifth-generation AI chip, known as the AI5, which Musk has said is specifically designed to be the “brain” behind Tesla’s most advanced technologies.
Article Summary: US chipmaker will bring its expertise in designing, fabricating and packaging high-performance chips to the table. The ultimate goal is to help Terafab achieve a production rate of 1 Terawatt (TW) of computing power per year, which will be used to fuel the next generation of artificial intelligence (AI) and robotics.

Article Title: Joanne Jang, who led GPT-4, Dall-E 2 and other top ChatGPT projects at OpenAI, quits: Read her note to employees
Article Text:
Credit: Time Magazine

Jang has legacy of major AI releases at OpenAI



Read full note she sent to employees on Slack



Hi! After 4.5 years, I'm leaving OpenAI.

I joined when OpenAI was still a research lab under 200 people. It's felt like I've worked at at least three different companies since then, and I've had so much fun in each one.

I got to help bring GPT-4, DALL-E 2, Text-to-Speech, Chat API, and Memory to the world. I got to build model behavior as a discipline -- from designing personality for 4o, 4.5, and 03 through post-training / RL to building eval flywheels grounded in real-world use. I'm especially proud of how we fought for user freedom & transparency, even when it was the harder call. And most recently, I got to start OpenAI Labs, where the team's been cooking up genuinely refreshing interfaces for people & AI [more in thread

... But if I were to be remembered for one thing, I hope it's that I brought froges and pink text to OpenAI.

Whatever dent I was able to make was because people took a chance on me and on work that was exploratory, creative, and not always obviously valuable at first.

Leadership made room for it and protected it. People in Model Behavior & Labs left their very real jobs to build something from nothing with me -- becoming the first-ever model designers, inventing new ways for people to interact with AI, etc. They're some of the most curious, creative, courageous, and mischievous people I know, and I've learned so much from them.

OpenAI has never been a normal company, and I hope it never becomes too normal. I've loved that our culture allows for thinking and doing things in unconventional ways. Please keep doing that - I'll be rooting for you all!

With lots and lots and lots of love,

Joanne





Joanne Jang, a key executive who helped shape some of the top capabilities and personality of the world’s most popular artificial intelligence (AI) tools, has announced her departure from OpenAI after four and a half years. Jang served as the company’s Head of Model Behavior and was recognized on the TIME100 AI 2025 list. She shared the news of her resignation in a heartfelt, nostalgic message to her colleagues on the company’s internal Slack channel.Jang joined OpenAI when it was still a relatively small research lab with fewer than 200 employees. During her tenure, she witnessed massive corporate growth and played a role in bringing some of the company's most famous products to the public. Her key contributions included work on:GPT-4 and the Chat API, DALL-E 2 image generation, Text-to-Speech and Memory features for ChatGPT.
Article Summary: Joanne Jang is leaving OpenAI after four and a half years. Jang served as the company’s Head of Model Behavior and was recognized on the TIME100 AI 2025 list. She shared the news of her resignation in a heartfelt, nostalgic message to her colleagues.

Article Title: Google Photos updates Pixel-exclusive AI editing feature: What it means for users
Article Text:
Representative Image

What’s changing in Google Photos app’s Pixel-exclusive AI Enhance feature



“AI Enhance I”

“AI Enhance II.”

“AI Enhance II”

“AI Enhance I”

“choice fatigue,”





Google Photos is reportedly testing an update to one of its Pixel-exclusive AI editing tools. The company is reportedly changing how users interact with the AI-based image editing tools. The update focuses on the AI Enhance feature, which currently generates multiple edited versions of a photo. Instead of expanding its capabilities, Google appears to be restructuring the feature into separate variants with fewer output choices. The move could influence how users edit photos on Pixel devices, particularly those who rely on automated tools for quick adjustments. The feature is still in testing and has not been rolled out widely.According to a report by Android Authority, Google Photos’ Pixel-exclusive AI Enhance tool which uses AI to automatically adjust elements such as composition, lighting, and colours currently generates three different versions of an edited image, allowing users to pick one based on their preference. However, that is going to change:The update splits this feature into two versions:andWhilecontinues to offer multiple output options, the newerproduces only a single edited image. This change could be aimed at reducing what is often referred to aswhere users may find it difficult to select from several similar outputs.The report suggests that limiting the number of generated images may also reduce computational load on Google’s systems. However, early observations indicate that generating a single image does not significantly reduce processing time compared to producing three versions.The feature has been spotted in testing through an APK teardown of version 7.69.0.890655694 of the app. It is currently available to a small group of users and has not yet been released widely. Apart from the change in the number of outputs, there are no noticeable differences in how the edits are applied between the two versions.As with most features identified through teardowns, it remains unclear whether the update will be rolled out broadly. However, it's important to note that Google has not officially confirmed the changes or provided details on its long-term plans for the AI Enhance feature.
Article Summary: Google Photos is reportedly testing an update to one of its Pixel-exclusive AI editing tools. The company is reportedly changing how users interact with the AI-based tool. The update focuses on the AI Enhance feature, which currently generates multiple edited versions of a photo. Instead of expanding its capabilities, Google appears to be restructuring the feature into separate variants.

Article Title: Quote of the day by PayPal co-founder Peter Thiel: “Madness is rare in individuals—but in groups, parties, nations, and ages it is the rule”
Article Text:
Quote of the day by PayPal co-founder Peter Thiel

Quote of the day by PayPal co-founder Peter Thiel



“Madness is rare in individuals—but in groups, parties, nations, and ages it is the rule”

Understanding the meaning behind the quote by Peter Thiel



Why group behaviour can become irrational



Historical examples that reflect this idea



The role of modern technology and social media



The importance of independent thinking



A closer look at human psychology



Relevance of the quote in everyday life



Other famous quotes by Peter Thiel



“The most contrarian thing of all is not to oppose the crowd but to think for yourself.”

“Brilliant thinking is rare, but courage is in even shorter supply than genius.”

“Every moment in business happens only once.”

“Secrets are the roots of all innovation.”

Lessons to take away







Quotes often express complicated ideas in a few short words. They make us stop and think about how people act and think, both as individuals and as part of a larger group. Today's quote is from Peter Thiel, a well-known entrepreneur and investor who has talked a lot about how important it is to think for yourself and the dangers of going along with what everyone else is doing. His words point out a pattern that has been seen throughout history: people can act rationally on their own, but groups can make decisions that seem crazy or extreme. This notion is not novel; it persists in contemporary relevance, particularly in an era influenced by social media, political movements, and collective trends. We can learn more about how people act and make decisions by figuring out what this quote means.The saying "Madness is rare in individuals—but in groups, parties, nations, and ages it is the rule" shows how people act differently when they are in a group. People often think carefully, weigh their options, and act in a measured way when they're alone. But their behavior can change when they are part of a group.People may feel less responsible for what they do when they are with others. This can cause them to make choices they wouldn't normally make.The quote says that when people act together, they are more likely to do things that don't make sense or are too extreme. This can happen in political movements, social trends, or even when people talk online.The point isn't to call groups bad, but to show how group dynamics can change how people think. It reminds people that sometimes groups don't act in a logical or reasonable way.There are a few reasons why groups don't always act the same way as individuals. One important thing is how people tend to follow others. It's easier to believe in something when a lot of people around us do too.Another reason is how emotions affect people. Feelings can spread quickly in a group. When more people feel the same way, their excitement, fear, or anger can grow stronger. This can cause people to make decisions based on how they feel instead of how they think.Peer pressure is also a factor. People often want to blend in and not stand out. So, they might agree with the group even if they aren't sure. This can lead to a situation where the group goes in a direction that no one person planned.There are many times in history when group behavior led to outcomes that were not expected. Big movements, revolutions, and mass reactions often show how quickly people's minds can change.At different times, whole societies have backed ideas that were later called into question or thrown out. These examples show how group thinking can have a big effect on decisions. Even though people in those groups may have had different opinions, the group's overall direction usually won out.This doesn't mean that all group actions are bad. Working together has also led to many good changes. However, the quote highlights the importance of being aware of how group influence works.Technology has made it easier to see and move quickly in groups in today's world. Ideas can spread in seconds on social media sites. A single post can reach millions of people, and people can react quickly.Sometimes, this setting can make group thinking stronger. People may be more likely to agree with an idea if they see a lot of other people supporting it without thinking about it more. Conversations online can get heated, with strong feelings affecting what people say.Technology also gives people more information than ever before, though. This gives you a chance to think for yourself, even when you're part of bigger conversations.One of the main points of the quote is how important it is to think for yourself. It's normal and often helpful to be part of a group, but it's also important to question ideas and form your own opinions.Independent thinking does not entail dismissing all collective opinions. Instead, it means carefully checking information before you believe it. This method can help people not be swayed by short-lived trends or feelings.This can mean doing simple things in everyday life, like checking facts, thinking about different points of view, and taking time to think before making a choice.Individual reasoning and social influence both affect how people act. For a long time, psychologists have looked at how people act when they are in groups. Ideas like "groupthink" show how wanting everyone in a group to get along can lead to bad decisions.In these kinds of situations, people in the group might not say they disagree in order to keep the group together. This can lead to decisions that aren't fully thought out over time. The quote shows this idea by saying that group thinking can sometimes go against reason.People can learn more about their own behavior by understanding these patterns. It helps people see when a group is affecting them and make better choices.The quote's meaning goes beyond just big political or social events. You can also see it in everyday life. People might follow fashion, entertainment, or opinions just because they are popular.In the workplace, the most powerful people in a meeting can sometimes change the group's decision. Peer pressure can affect what people do and what they choose to do in schools and colleges. The need to agree with others can affect how people make decisions, even in small groups.People can take steps to think more clearly and act with more awareness if they know about these things.The quote is a simple but important reminder. Groups can bring people together and make things better, but they can also change how people think in ways that don't always make sense.People can make better choices when they know how groups act. This means weighing the pros and cons of working together with the need to make your own decisions.In a world that changes quickly, where information spreads quickly and opinions can change quickly, being able to think for yourself is even more important.Peter Thiel's quote captures an idea that has been true throughout history. It shows the difference between how people think and how groups act, and how group dynamics can have a big effect on decisions. The quote reminds us to stay aware and thoughtful, even though being part of a group is a normal part of life. Individuals can better handle difficult situations when they combine the power of working together with their own ideas.
Article Summary: Quote of the day by PayPal co-founder Peter Thiel: "Madness is rare in individuals, but in groups, nations, and ages it is the rule" We can learn more about how people act and make decisions by figuring out what this quote means. The quote highlights the importance of being aware of how group influence works.

Article Title: American technology industry's biggest rivals Google, OpenAI and Anthropic come together to fight Silicon Valley's 'Chinese problem' that they recently warned government of
Article Text:
Three of America's biggest tech rivals—OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google—are sharing intelligence through the Frontier Model Forum to combat distillation attacks from Chinese AI labs. Anthropic has named DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax as having run industrial-scale extraction campaigns against Claude using 24,000 fraudulent accounts. Both companies have flagged the threat to US lawmakers, framing unauthorized distillation as a national security risk, not just a commercial one.

What distillation actually is, and why it's become a problem

Beyond the business case: why Washington is paying attention

The distillation fight is really about who controls the next decade of AI





Three of America's most competitive AI companies are now sharing intelligence with each other—not about their models, but about who's trying to steal them. OpenAI , Anthropic, and Google have begun quietly coordinating to detect and block Chinese AI firms from siphoning capabilities out of their systems through a technique called distillation. The effort runs through the Frontier Model Forum, an industry nonprofit all three founded alongside Microsoft in 2023, Bloomberg reported.It's an unusual arrangement—these are companies that fight over the same customers, the same engineers, and increasingly, the same government dollars. But the threat has apparently grown large enough to set rivalries aside.Distillation is standard practice in AI development. Companies use it routinely to build smaller, cheaper versions of their own models. What's not standard is when a competitor uses the same technique—at industrial scale, through fake accounts and proxy networks—to clone your model's capabilities without paying for the R&D behind them.Anthropic has put specific numbers to this. In February, the company named three Chinese labs—DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax—as having run coordinated extraction campaigns against Claude, racking up over 16 million exchanges through roughly 24,000 fraudulent accounts.MiniMax alone drove more than 13 million of those. To get around Anthropic's ban on commercial access from China, the labs allegedly routed traffic through proxy services running networks of up to 20,000 fake accounts at a time, mixing extraction traffic with ordinary requests to avoid detection.OpenAI told the US House Select Committee on China in February that DeepSeek had kept up similar efforts against American labs through increasingly obfuscated methods. Google's threat intelligence team flagged a surge in what it calls "model extraction attacks" on Gemini—one campaign alone generated over 100,000 prompts apparently designed to replicate the model's chain-of-thought reasoning.Both Anthropic and OpenAI have been careful to frame this beyond lost revenue. Models built through unauthorized distillation, they say, tend to shed the safety guardrails US labs spend heavily to build in—limits on things like bioweapon synthesis instructions or large-scale cyberattack assistance. Feed those stripped-down models into military or intelligence systems, and the problem compounds fast.US officials estimated to Bloomberg, on condition of anonymity, that unauthorized distillation costs Silicon Valley labs billions in annual profit. The Trump administration's AI Action Plan has called for a formal information-sharing center partly to address this—suggesting at least some appetite in Washington to put structure around what is currently a quiet, informal arrangement between rivals.The information-sharing effort is a start, but it papers over a structural problem. As long as proxy networks can spin up 20,000 fake accounts faster than any one company can shut them down, the math doesn't work in the defenders' favour. OpenAI has explicitly called for an "ecosystem security" approach—hardening not just individual labs but API routers, cloud providers, and payment infrastructure simultaneously. One weak link is enough.Meanwhile, the attacks are getting harder to spot. Chinese actors have moved well past simple output scraping into multi-stage pipelines that blend synthetic data generation with reinforcement learning—essentially automating the process of building a rival model on someone else's foundation. Anthropic caught MiniMax mid-campaign and watched it pivot within 24 hours of a new Claude release, redirecting traffic to capture capabilities from the latest version. That kind of operational speed is difficult to counter without coordination that currently doesn't exist at scale.
Article Summary: OpenAI, Anthropic, and Google are sharing intelligence through the Frontier Model Forum to combat distillation attacks from Chinese AI labs. Anthropic has named DeepSeek, Moonshot, and MiniMax as having run industrial-scale extraction campaigns against Claude using 24,000 fraudulent accounts.

Article Title: Oracle CFO Hilary Maxson: Who is Hilary Maxson: Oracle's new Chief Financial Officer’s educational background, career journey, and expected salary
Article Text:
PC: LinkedIn

Hilary Maxson's early career in banking and financial services



Hilary Maxson’s global leadership experience at The AES Corporation



Hilary Maxson career at Schneider Electric and global CFO leadership role



Hilary Maxson board role at Anglo American and education background



Hilary Maxson appointment at Oracle



Hilary Maxson's salary at Oracle







Hilary Maxson’s recent appointment as the chief financial officer of Oracle Corporation is another milestone in her career that spans different continents and sectors with complex financial dynamics. Unlike many professionals who pursue careers along the same trajectory, Hilary’s career has taken various turns, involving banking, energy, infrastructure, and international leadership of big corporations. Through her career, she has gradually assumed more strategic positions, most of which have been associated with big projects or companies that require intensive financial investments.Her career path matches well with Oracle Corporation’s present course, especially its investments in building new data centers as well as its development in the artificial intelligence sector. Before joining Oracle Corporation, she developed a long career at Schneider Electric SE as an executive financier.In New York, Hilary Maxson began her career in financial services at several big firms. These include Bank of America as well as Citi. These were jobs involving trading, sales analysis, and business development on successful teams.Maxson then entered the energy field, where she joined The AES Corporation and stayed there for more than ten years.She had various roles at this company during this time period. These roles include the Managing Director of M&A, CFO for Africa, and then CFO for Asia. These roles required her to oversee finance-related activities in various regions, which included dealing with such issues as project financing, treasuries, budgets, and risks.In particular, she had to deal with financing complicated infrastructure projects, including power generation in Africa and discussions with government bodies.Maxson’s career at AES also gave her international exposure across North America, South America, Africa, and Asia. She led finance teams in multiple geographies and worked on large-scale energy projects.Her roles were not limited to accounting or reporting. They included investment decisions, strategic planning, and supporting operational leadership. She was part of senior leadership teams and contributed to investment committees, suggesting involvement in broader corporate strategy rather than just finance operations.This global experience appears to have shaped her ability to manage distributed teams and complex organisational structures. It also reflects familiarity with industries where infrastructure, long-term capital deployment, and regulatory frameworks play a major role.Maxson moved to Schneider Electric in 2017, and there she took up more responsibilities than before. She occupied various positions within the senior ranks of the company's finance leaders prior to joining the position of Executive Vice President & Group CFO in 2020.At Schneider Electric, she supervised global finance-related activities such as accounting, tax affairs, treasury activities, investor relations and M&A activities. The scope of her responsibilities extended to thousands of employees from different departments. In addition, she worked on transformation programs with an emphasis on digitalisation and business improvement.Some of the key areas that she covered in her capacity at Schneider Electric included energy management, building systems and industrial technology. Schneider Electric, being in energy and infrastructure seems to be consistent with Maxson's earlier experience with large projects.Alongside her executive responsibilities, Hilary Maxson has also held non-executive roles. She serves as a non-executive director at Anglo American, where she chairs the audit committee and participates in governance-related committees, contributing to oversight, risk management, and strategic financial guidance across multiple board initiatives.Her academic background includes a degree from Cornell University and an MBA from the Cornell Johnson Graduate School of Management. These credentials are consistent with her early entry into finance roles and later progression into senior leadership positions, reflecting a strong foundation in analytical thinking, corporate finance principles, leadership development, and long-term strategic decision-making within complex organizational environments.Her appointment at Oracle comes at a time when the company is investing heavily in AI infrastructure and expanding its cloud capabilities. Maxson’s background in infrastructure-heavy industries and global finance operations appears to match the demands of this phase. From banking desks to energy projects to multinational corporate finance, the pattern is clear enough.Now, at Oracle, that experience is being applied to one of the most capital-intensive technology strategies in the industry.As reported by The CSR Journal, Maxson, aged 48 and formerly of Schneider Electric, has assumed the role effective immediately, with an expected annual base salary of $950,000 and eligibility for a performance-based bonus with a target value of $2.5 million. According to an official Oracle filing, the bonus is contingent on her meeting defined performance metrics and will be prorated for the current financial year ending May 31, 2026, bringing her total potential cash compensation to $3.45 million, excluding stock incentives.
Article Summary: Hilary Maxson's career has taken various turns, involving banking, energy, infrastructure, and international leadership of big corporations. Before joining Oracle Corporation, she developed a long career at Schneider Electric SE as an executive financier. Her career path matches well with Oracle’s present course, especially its investments in building new data centers.

Article Title: AI music startup Suno hits deadlock in licensing talks with Universal Music and Sony Music: Report
Article Text:
Key disagreement over music distribution





AI music startup Suno is facing a deadlock in licensing talks with major record labels, exposing sharp divisions over how the music industry should handle AI-generated content. According to a report by the Financial Times, discussions between Suno and leading music companies, including Universal Music and Sony Music, have made little progress in recent months. No agreement has been reached so far, as both sides remain divided on key terms, the report said. “We have ongoing engagement, but there is no path forward with the current proposal,” a person involved in the negotiations told the publication.For those unaware, Suno is one of the most popular AI music companies. The platform allows users to create songs within seconds using simple text prompts. The company was valued at $2.45 billion last year and has around 2 million paying subscribers.At the centre of the dispute is how AI-generated songs should be used after they are created. Universal wants such tracks to remain within apps like Suno and not be freely shared across the internet.Suno, however, wants users to be able to distribute and share their creations more widely.Record labels argue that AI tools like Suno rely on music created by human artists and should pay for using that content. However, both sides have not been able to agree on how such payments should work. Suno said it remains open to working with the industry. “We want to work cooperatively with the music industry to unlock new sources of revenue for artists,” the company said.In June 2024, Universal, Sony and Warner sued Suno and rival Udio over copyright infringement. Suno then responded by criticising the lawsuits, with CEO Mikey Shulman accusing record labels of “reverting to their old lawyer-led playbook.”Earlier this year, a group of artists and industry organisations also urged the music industry to reject Suno, claiming its technology uses artists’ work without permission.The standoff comes at a time when music companies are under pressure, with shares of major labels falling to three-year lows amid concerns over AI. Investors are closely watching these talks, as licensing deals could determine whether AI becomes a threat or a new source of growth for the music industry.
Article Summary: Suno is one of the most popular AI music companies. The platform allows users to create songs within seconds using simple text prompts. The company was valued at $2.45 billion last year and has around 2 million paying subscribers. Universal, Sony and Warner sued Suno and rival Udio over copyright infringement.

Article Title: What is the Microsoft Windows game that Iran is ‘using’ to tease Donald Trump
Article Text:
What happened

Watch 'WILL UNLEASH DEVASTATION': Iran Bombs UAE Telecom Center In First Strike On Communication Facility 'WILL UNLEASH DEVASTATION': Iran Bombs UAE Telecom Center In First Strike On Communication Facility

What is Minesweeper game

Why Iran has used Minesweeper





The tensions between the US and Iran have taken a digital turn. The war of words is no longer limited to official press conferences and speeches; it has spilled onto the social media, where Iranian embassies around the world are taking potshots at the comments made by US President Donald Trump . In one of the responses, the Iranian Embassy in Mazar-i-Sharif, Afghanistan, used a classic 90s computer game to ‘mock’ Trump’s efforts to open the Strait of Hormuz.In recent days, Iranian embassy accounts across the globe have launched a coordinated wave of sharply worded online attacks. These are designed to mock US President Trump’s language used in one of this posts on Truth Social. Arguably, the one of most viral examples came from the Iranian consulate in Mazar-e-Sharif, Afghanistan. The embassy posted a satirical video aimed directly at making fun of Trump’s efforts to open the Strait of Hormuz.The video overlays gameplay from the classic Microsoft Windows game, Minesweeper, onto a satellite map of the waterway, which is a highly contested and critical global shipping route. As the video plays, viewers watch repeated “mine” explosions go off across the grid in the Strait. The video features sarcastic text praising Trump's attempts as “commendable.”If you owned a computer in the 1990s or early 2000s, you will surely remember this classic game.Minesweeper is a single-player puzzle game that comes pre-installed on almost every Microsoft Windows computer. The goal of the game board is a grid of blank gray squares, and hidden underneath some of those squares are “mines” (bombs). The goal is to click and clear every safe square on the board without accidentally clicking on a mine.When players click on a safe square, it reveals a number that tells them exactly how many hidden bombs are touching that specific square. By using logic and these number clues, you have to deduce where the bombs are hidden and avoid them. If you click on a square that hides a mine, the bomb explodes, the game is over, and you lose immediately.The reason to use Minesweeper to bring humour and mocking serves a clear political purpose. Iran has announced that it has installed mines in the Strait of Hormuz as a way to stop ships and tankers from passing through without a green light from the Iranian authorities. Minesweeper essentially implies that any US navigation through the waters is as risky as clicking a bomb in the game.
Article Summary: Iranian embassies around the world are taking potshots at the comments made by US President Donald Trump. In one of the responses, the Iranian Embassy in Mazar-i-Sharif, Afghanistan, used a classic 90s computer game to ‘mock’ Trump’s efforts to open the Strait of Hormuz.

Article Title: Yatra brings Google's Gemini AI to travel booking and expense management
Article Text:
DIYA books flights, manages cancellations, and stays within your company's travel policy

RECAP can now scan receipts and log expenses by voice





Yatra has partnered with Google Cloud to overhaul how Indians plan, book, and expense business travel—using Gemini on Vertex AI as the engine behind it all.The tie-up puts Google's multimodal AI at the center of two of Yatra's core products: DIYA , its conversational booking agent, and RECAP, its corporate expense tool. Both are being rebuilt to do more than surface information—they're designed to actually execute tasks.DIYA uses Gemini's natural language capabilities to handle multi-step travel requests in a single conversation. Ask it to find a flight, check it against your company's policy, and complete the booking—it strings those steps together via Function Calling, which lets it talk directly to Yatra's internal APIs in real-time. Retrieval-Augmented Generation keeps its answers grounded in Yatra's own travel databases and FAQs rather than generic internet results, which matters when policy compliance is involved.On the expense side, RECAP gets Gemini's vision analytics and OCR. Point your phone at a receipt, and it pulls out the relevant data automatically. Employees can also log expenses by voice—a practical upgrade for anyone filing a hotel bill at 11 PM after a long day of meetings.Yatra CEO Siddhartha Gupta called it a move toward "a truly integrated travel ecosystem." The infrastructure runs entirely on Google Cloud, with Yatra's enterprise data kept out of Google's public model training pipeline.
Article Summary: Yatra has partnered with Google Cloud to overhaul how Indians plan, book, and expense business travel. The tie-up puts Google's multimodal AI at the center of two of Yatra's core products. DIYA uses Gemini's natural language capabilities to handle multi-step travel requests.

Article Title: Intel appoints Santhosh Viswanathan to lead Asia Pacific and Japan
Article Text: Intel Corporation has announced the appointment of Santhosh Viswanathan as vice president and managing director for the Asia Pacific and Japan (APJ) region, expanding his leadership scope from India to cover one of the company’s most diverse and fast-growing markets.In his new role, Viswanathan will oversee Intel’s overall business, brand, and customer engagements across APJ, bringing the region under a unified leadership structure. He will be responsible for driving growth, strengthening partnerships, and advancing Intel’s strategic priorities across multiple geographies.Viswanathan has been with Intel for over two decades, holding leadership positions across India, Southeast Asia, Australia, New Zealand, and the United States. Most recently, he served as Vice President and Managing Director for Intel India, where he played a pivotal role in positioning the country as a key growth engine for the company. Earlier, he was Global Director of Operations in the U.S., leading sales operations strategy and planning for Intel’s global sales organization.“APJ is one of the most diverse and fast-growing regions in the world, and its strength lies in its people, partners, and technology ecosystems,” said Viswanathan. “I am excited to take on this expanded responsibility and work closely with our teams and customers to unlock new opportunities, drive growth, and create lasting impact across the region.”Viswanathan holds a degree in Industrial Engineering and Management and an MBA, equipping him with both technical and business expertise to lead Intel’s operations in APJ.
Article Summary: Santhosh Viswanathan will be responsible for driving growth, strengthening partnerships, and advancing Intel’s strategic priorities across multiple geographies. He has been with Intel for over two decades, holding leadership positions across India, Southeast Asia, Australia, New Zealand, and the United States.

Article Title: Microsoft responds to viral claims on Copilot's terms of service language; says: 'Entertainment purposes' phrasing is ...
Article Text:
Microsoft explains legacy language role in the viral controversy

How competitors frame their AI tools





Microsoft has now clarified that its Copilot Terms of Use after viral posts highlighted a clause stating the AI tool was “for entertainment purposes only.” The phrasing which appeared inconsistent with how the company has marketed Copilot as a productivity and enterprise solution, quickly drew attention online and raised questions about Microsoft’s confidence in its flagship AI product.In a statement first published by PCMag, a Microsoft spokesperson explained: “The ‘entertainment purposes’ phrasing is legacy language from when Copilot originally launched as a search companion service in Bing. As the product has evolved, that language is no longer reflective of how Copilot is used today and will be altered with our next update.”The company further stressed that Copilot’s current role is integrated into Microsoft 365 and enterprise workflows which goes far beyond entertainment. CEO Satya Nadella recently praised Copilot’s accuracy and latency during the January earnings call, underscoring its importance to Microsoft’s AI strategy.Microsoft’s wording stands out compared to rivals. OpenAI, Anthropic, Meta, and xAI all include disclaimers about AI limitations but avoid the “entertainment purposes” phrasing. For example, OpenAI’s terms caution users not to rely on outputs as a sole source of truth, while Meta explicitly prohibits using AI outputs for regulated activities like medical or financial advice.Elon Musk’s xAI goes further, requiring users to indemnify the company against liability.The “entertainment” clause dates back to early Bing Chat terms in 2023, before Microsoft rebranded the service as Copilot. Observers note that quirky disclaimers are not new for Microsoft — some have pointed to humorous clauses in past software licenses, including Windows NT in the 1990s.
Article Summary: Microsoft clarifies Copilot Terms of Use after viral posts highlighted a clause stating the AI tool was “for entertainment purposes only” The phrasing appeared inconsistent with how the company has marketed Copilot as a productivity and enterprise solution. CEO Satya Nadella recently praised Copilot’s accuracy and latency during the January earnings call.
